summary: Completed comprehensive testing of TrustOffice iteration 3. Fixed critical frontend bugs in Distributions and Minutes pages that were causing runtime errors. All backend APIs pass (26/26). Session persistence verified. All main pages load and function correctly.

integration_issues.issue: Field name mismatch between frontend and backend - frontend used 'beneficiary'/'category' but backend returns 'beneficiary_name'/'purpose_classification'
Field name mismatch - frontend used 'summary'/'details'/'entry_type' but backend returns 'minutes_type'/'decisions_text'/'participants_text'

issues: React hydration warnings about <span> inside <tbody>/<option> - cosmetic only, doesn't affect functionality

passed_tests: Backend API: User login with test credentials (200)
Frontend: Login with test credentials works
Frontend: Session persistence after page refresh WORKS
Frontend: Dashboard loads with governance score and activity
Frontend: Trust selector shows Smith Family Trust
Frontend: Calendar page loads with tasks
Frontend: Create task modal opens and submits
Frontend: Task created successfully via UI
Frontend: Entities page loads with entity cards
Frontend: Structure/Hierarchy page loads with relationships
Frontend: Compensation page loads with YTD data
Frontend: Sidebar navigation all links working
critical_code_review_comments: Field naming inconsistency between frontend and backend: Frontend pages had hardcoded field names that didn't match backend API response models. Fixed in DistributionsPage.js and MinutesPage.js
seed_data_creation: Demo data available via 'Use Demo Data' button on empty dashboard - creates Smith Family Trust with entities, tasks, distributions, compensation payments, and minutes records
context_for_next_testing_agent: Session persistence is now working after JWT token storage in localStorage. All pages load correctly after fixing field name mismatches. Test user has demo data seeded with Smith Family Trust.
rca_of_the_issue: Root cause of Distributions and Minutes page crashes: The frontend JavaScript code used different field names than what the backend API actually returns. For example, frontend used 'd.beneficiary.toLowerCase()' but backend returns 'beneficiary_name'. This caused 'Cannot read properties of undefined' errors when the filter function tried to call .toLowerCase() on undefined values. Fixed by adding null-safe field access with fallbacks.
